data.title: Anglo American to sell Brazil nickel business for up to $500M, keep 20% of demerged platinum unit
data.description:
Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said Tuesday it agreed to sell its nickel business in Brazil to Hong Kong-listed MMG Ltd. for as much as $500M, the latest step in the company's broader restructuring aimed at refocusing its operations on copper and iron ore mining.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said the deal comprises a $350M up-front cash payment at completion, up to $100M in a price-linked earnout and a contingent $50M in cash for the potential development of the projects.

Anglo's (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) nickel business includes two ferronickel operations and two growth projects in Brazil, producing a combined ~38K metric tons/year of nickel.

Ahead of its financial results on February 20, Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) also said the spinoff of its platinum unit in South Africa will be completed by June; the company plans to keep a 19.9% stake but will not hold any representation on Anglo American Platinum's (OTCPK:ANGPY) (OTCPK:AGPPF) board after the split.

The miner, which already disposed of its steelmaking coal business, also is trying to sell its De Beers diamond unit, but the business is proving hard to sell and is expected to book an impairment on its full-year results.

Anglo's (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) decision to retain a stake after the demerger is not the clean break analysts had expected, RBC Capital analysts Ben Davis and Marina Calero wrote.

data.title: Odd Burger CEO purchases 50,000 company shares
data.description:
• Odd Burger (TSXV:ODD:CA) said on Tuesday that Co-founder and Chief Operating Officer, Vasiliki McInnes purchased 50,000 shares of the company at an average price of $0.3231 per share.
  • As a result of the acquisition, McInnes will now own and exercise control over an ‎aggregate of ‎‎22.46M ‎‎common shares, 1.84M options ‎and 892,857 warrants representing ‎approximately 22.8% ‎of the issued ‎and outstanding voting ‎securities of the ‎‎issuer on a non-diluted ‎basis and 25.6% of the ‎issued and outstanding securities of ‎the issuer, ‎‎assuming conversion of the ‎options and ‎exercise of the warrants‎. ‎
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock futures higher as Wall Street kicks off holiday-shortened week
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were higher on Tuesday with attention on geopolitics.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were +0.46% in pre-market trading, while benchmark S&P futures (SPX) were +0.39%. Dow futures (INDU) were +0.19%.

There was more action in the bond market as global rates rose. European bonds were the driver, with prices falling on speculation that the market would see new issuance as countries spent more on defense.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.53% and the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up nearly 1 basis point to 4.29%.

The U.S. and Russia peace talks over Ukraine are set to begin today in Saudi Arabia. But Ukraine and European nations will not be present.

Deutsche Bank strategist Jim Reid said "potential implications are huge after the events of the last five days where first the US froze out Europe in announcing the start of talks with Russia, and second with the broadside against Europe from US Vice President JD Vance."

"That all this is occurring within a week of the German general election (this Sunday) means that Europe might start to move down a very different path within weeks to the pre-existing one."

On the macro front, the February Empire State Manufacturing Index surprised to the upside.

"The initial reaction (Monday) was to price in more European defense spending with bonds selling off and equities rising, especially in the defense sector," Reid said.

data.title: ProPhase Labs hires former Barstool Sports COO Stuart Hollenshead, plans cost-cutting measures
data.description:
ProPhase Labs (NASDAQ:PRPH) Tuesday named Stu Hollenshead as Chief Operating Officer, marking a pivotal step in the company’s expansion into consumer-centered health and wellness products.

ProPhase Labs (NASDAQ:PRPH) said it plans to provide additional updates very shortly regarding progress in its accounts receivables that the company believes will be significant, the exploration of new strategic alternatives for Nebula Genomics and DNA Complete, and additional cost-cutting measures.

“The company’s particular focus going forward will be direct-to-consumer revenue streams," Ted Karkus, CEO of ProPhase Labs said.

Furthermore, Karkus said the company is "optimistic about the potential for a significant inflow of capital into the company between Q2 and Q3 2025" related to enhanced accounts receivable collection initiatives.

"These are just some of the reasons that someone of Stu’s caliber was willing to transition from being a consultant at ProPhase to its COO," Karkus added.

Hollenshead served as Chief Operating Officer & Chief Business Officer at Barstool Sports, leading the company through record-breaking expansion in revenue, audience, and product monetization—culminating in its $551-million acquisition by Penn Entertainment in 2023.

data.title: Plus Therapeutics secures $5.7M to support leptomeningeal metastases program
data.description:
• Plus Therapeutics (NASDAQ:PSTV) said on Tuesday that it has closed a private placement with aggregate initial upfront proceeds of approximately $3.7M.
  • The private placement comprises of the issuance and sale of secured convertible promissory notes, in the aggregate amount of $3.3M and common stock purchase warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of about 3M shares of the company's common stock at an exercise price of $1.12 per share.
  • The notes have a maturity date of one year from the closing of the financing and are convertible, under certain circumstances, to shares of common stock of the company at an exercise price of $1.12 per share.
  • The notes bear interest at the rate of 10% per annum, payable on the last business day of each quarter.
  • The accompanying warrants will be exercisable until the five-year anniversary of the closing of the financing.
  • In addition, the company has received a $2M advance payment from CPRIT as part of its existing $17.6M grant award.
  • About $5.2M remains from the original $17.6 million grant award.
  • The funding from the private placement and the CPRIT advance will support the company’s clinical development of Rhenium (186Re) Obisbemeda for leptomeningeal metastases (LM), as well as further development of the company’s CNSide LM diagnostic test as a pivotal trial endpoint, the company said.
  • On February 13, 2025, the company also issued secured convertible promissory notes in the aggregate amount of $3.1M in exchange for existing investors agreeing to cancel 3.5M Series A common stock purchase warrants issued by the company in May 2024.
  • PSTV +4.03% premarket to $1.29.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Empire State Manufacturing Index turns positive in February, suprising to upside
data.description:
February Empire State Manufacturing Index: +5.7 vs. -0.5 consensus and -12.6 prior.

New Orders: 11.4 vs. -8.6 in January.

Shipments : 14.2 vs. -1.7 prior.

Price Paid: 11.1 vs. +29.1.

data.title: Revolve Renewable Power secures $1.5 million from project sale
data.description:
• Revolve Renewable Power (OTCQB:REVVF) on Tuesday announced the sale of a 3MW combined heat and power project from its distributed generation portfolio for $1.5 million.
  • The company said the proceeds from the sale will be reflected as revenues in its Q3 FY2025 financial results.
  • The combined heat and power generator unit (the "CHP Project") was originally acquired by Revolve in August 2022 as part of the $1.4m acquisition of Centrica Business Solutions Mexico.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Light & Wonder to acquire charitable gaming assets of Grover Gaming for $850M cash
data.description:
• Light & Wonder (NASDAQ:LNW) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire the charitable gaming assets of Grover Gaming and G2 Gaming (together, “Grover Gaming”) for $850M cash.
  • The deal also includes a four-year revenue-based earn-out of up to $200M in cash.
  • Grover Charitable Gaming has an installed base of over 10,000 leased electronic pull-tab units across five U.S. States, operating under a recurring revenue model.
  • In 2024 Grover Charitable Gaming generated revenue of $135M and Grover Adjusted EBITDA of $111M.
  • The company expects the transaction to close during Q2 2025.
  • Post acquisition, Light & Wonder will operate the business with a combination of Grover Gaming and Light & Wonder employees.
  • Light & Wonder expects to fund the acquisition with existing cash and incremental debt financing.
  • Post-acquisition, Light & Wonder expects its net debt leverage ratio on a combined basis to remain within its target range of 2.5x-3.5x.
  • Light & Wonder reaffirms its 2025 $1.4B consolidated AEBITDA target underpinned by continued strong organic growth across our existing businesses, and without any AEBITDA contribution from the acquisition of Grover Charitable Gaming.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Huawei launches tri-foldable phone Mate XT globally - report
data.description:
On Tuesday, Chinese tech giant Huawei held a global launch for the industry's first tri-foldable phone in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, the Associated Press reported.

The company's Mate XT, which was first launched in China in September last year, will have a price tag of €3,499 (about $3,660), the report added.

Huawei did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Mate XT, a Z-shaped phone and three panels, saw over 3 million pre-orders on the company's Vmall website in September 2024.

Huawei was once in competition with Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) to be the world's biggest handset maker until U.S. restrictions, starting in 2019, began to curb its access to chip manufacturing tools needed to produce its most advanced models.

However, in the past two years, Huawei has been making a comeback. In August 2023, the company surprised many by quietly launching its new flagship smartphone, Mate 60 Pro. Last year, the company launched its flagship Mate 70 smartphone, Mate XT, and the Pura 70 series.

Huawei and other Chinese smartphone makers are giving a tough challenge to Apple in China. Apple slipped to third spot in the Chinese market with a 17.1% market share, behind Huawei with an 18.1% share and second-placed Xiaomi with 17.2%, in the fourth quarter of 2024, according to Counterpoint Research.

However, Apple is also working on a foldable iPhone, which is currently in the planning stage.

data.title: Constellation Energy raises dividend by 10% to $0.3878
data.description:
• Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) declares $0.3878/share quarterly dividend, 10% increase from prior dividend of $0.3525.
  • Forward yield 0.51%
  • Payable March 18; for shareholders of record March 7; ex-div March 7.
  • See CEG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
